steps.title: Identify what the initial deposit represents
steps.content: The initial deposit is the account balance at the moment the account was opened. On a graph of $ y = f(x) $ where $ x $ is months since the initial deposit, this corresponds to $ x = 0 $. The initial deposit is the y-intercept of the line.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: Looking at the graph, trace the line to where it crosses the y-axis (at $ x = 0 $). The line intersects the y-axis at $ y = 10 $. Therefore, the initial deposit is $ \$10 $. That matches a value of 10.
